description:
You are the on-call backup operator and need to do a quick “table-top” restore test.

    Existing material on disk
      • An archive containing yesterday’s manifests: /home/user/archives/daily_backup.tar.gz  
        (inside the archive each manifest is stored under the path daily/, e.g. daily/valid_manifest.json).  
      • The JSON Schema you must validate against: /home/user/schemas/backup_manifest.schema.json  

    What you must achieve
      1. Create the directory /home/user/restore and unpack /home/user/archives/daily_backup.tar.gz into it, preserving the directory structure contained in the archive.  
      2. Use jq to validate every “*.json” file that ends up anywhere underneath /home/user/restore **against the supplied schema**.  
         (Hint: jq’s “-e” flag is useful because it sets the shell exit-status to non-zero when the filter returns false.)  
      3. Generate a plain-text log file at /home/user/validation/restore_report.log using exactly the format described below.  
      4. Do not write anything else anywhere; all verification relies solely on the contents of restore_report.log.  

    Required log format (strictly checked by the grader)
      • UNIX line-endings (LF, not CRLF).  
      • The file **must contain exactly five lines** for the supplied data set.  
      • Line-1  : the literal header text          RESTORE VALIDATION REPORT  
      • Line-2  : invalid_manifest.json: INVALID  
      • Line-3  : valid_manifest.json:   VALID    ← note two consecutive spaces before “VALID” are NOT required, just one; spacing before/after colon is free, but the words “INVALID” and “VALID” must be upper-case.  
      • Line-4  : a completely blank line (contains only the trailing newline).  
      • Line-5  : TOTAL=2 VALID=1 INVALID=1  

    Summary of what the autograder will look for
      1. /home/user/restore/daily/valid_manifest.json and /home/user/restore/daily/invalid_manifest.json must be present (extracted from the archive).  
      2. /home/user/validation/restore_report.log must exist and **match the five-line specification above byte-for-byte**.  
      3. No super-user privileges should be required; stay inside /home/user.  

    Complexity budget
      A correct solution typically needs 5-10 shell commands (mkdir, tar, jq, loops / finds for validation, redirection for the report, etc.).  You may of course use more if you like, but it is definitely possible in under ten commands.

    When you’re done, double-check that the required five-line log file exists and that the two manifests sit underneath /home/user/restore in the same sub-structure that was present in the tarball.
truth:
Pre-existing files & their exact contents
    ----------------------------------------

    1. /home/user/archives/daily_backup.tar.gz  
       The tarball contains two files stored with these paths **inside the archive**:

       a) daily/valid_manifest.json  
          {
            "backupId": "bku-20231015-01",
            "timestamp": "2023-10-15T02:15:30Z",
            "files": [
              { "filename": "etc/passwd", "checksum": "a54d88e06612d820bc3be72877c74f257b561b19" },
              { "filename": "etc/shadow", "checksum": "2c1743a391305fbf367df8e4f069f9f9a46b3be2" }
            ]
          }

       b) daily/invalid_manifest.json  
          {
            "backupId": "bku-20231015-02",
            "timestamp": "2023-10-15T02:16:00Z",
            "files": "should-be-array-but-is-string"
          }

    2. /home/user/schemas/backup_manifest.schema.json  
       {
         "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
         "type": "object",
         "required": ["backupId", "timestamp", "files"],
         "properties": {
           "backupId": { "type": "string" },
           "timestamp": { "type": "string", "format": "date-time" },
           "files": {
             "type": "array",
             "items": {
               "type": "object",
               "required": ["filename", "checksum"],
               "properties": {
                 "filename": { "type": "string" },
                 "checksum": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^[a-f0-9]{40}$" }
               }
             }
           }
         }
       }

    Directories that already exist before the agent starts:
      • /home/user/archives
      • /home/user/schemas

    Directories that DO NOT yet exist (the agent must create them):
      • /home/user/restore
      • /home/user/validation

    Expected artefact after the agent finishes:
      /home/user/validation/restore_report.log  — exact, byte-for-byte content must be:

      RESTORE VALIDATION REPORT
      invalid_manifest.json: INVALID
      valid_manifest.json: VALID
      
      TOTAL=2 VALID=1 INVALID=1

      (There is an LF at the end of every line, including the last one. Line-4 is a single LF.)

    The automated tests will:
      • confirm that /home/user/restore/daily/valid_manifest.json and /home/user/restore/daily/invalid_manifest.json exist.  
      • read /home/user/validation/restore_report.log, split on LF, and insist on the five lines matching the block above.  
      • ensure that no extra output files are created in /home/user/validation.
